page: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.3 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Geekbench text processing also demonstrates strong performance. However, multi-core and parallel processing metrics, such as Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the low core count. Memory bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average range, while database operations and Redis SET operations are relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r3-16 offers a high memory density per vCPU, allowing workloads requiring larger memory footprints to run without the expense of provisioning unnecessary CPU cores. Given the strong single-core performance but limited multi-core capacity and weak database operation benchmarks, this instance is best suited for memory-bound, single-threaded applications, lightweight caching layers, text processing pipelines, or small-scale memory-intensive utilities rather than high-throughput web serving or heavy parallel databases.
description: The OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high ratio of 8.0 GB of memory per core. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance includes a 50 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core execution and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is best suited for single-threaded memory-bound applications, text processing, and lightweight caching.
